entry.title: Mass Deletion of Contact Person - Customer Master Data
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

I need to delete/remove a contact person (with a dedicated functionID) from the customer master data (XD02). This should be done massively

I tried with an LSMW but there's no way to say to the recording the position that we want to delete/remove, it change from customer to customer.

Also checked with XD99 (MASS) transaction but there's only have the option to create/update and not to delete.

Any other idea how to do it in a "functional" way?

Kind regards

Joao Pereira

Hi ray buttigieg

Advise on below question:

Posting a clearing Document while adjusting invoic... - SAP Community

entry.title: Consuming on-prem OData service though Destination in python script and deploy it to CF in BTP.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have developed a python script in Business Application Studio in SAP BTP and in that I am trying to consume an on premise OData service through destination.
If am giving my backend Host & port URL I am able to consume the data directly, but it would not work if I deploy it as my service is not exposed outside.
Can you please help me how to call the OData service through destination in Python Script similar to how we call in SAP UI5 applications.

Thanks & Regards,

entry.title: Re: Create Extension Wizard to Create SAP UI5 Extension
entry.content:
Hi @Arushi ,

You are right, the app F2502 does not show up in the Adaptation wizard list. 

I checked the UI software package UIS4HOP1-600 and we do have it installed, so it should really come up in the list. And I even checked in SE80 and the BSP application is indeed installed in our system, so we do have the app in our system.

So... I think either SAP did not intend for this app to  be adaptable (which can't be right, since the Fiori App library shows a section for extensibility for this app), or  SAP's just missed putting this in the list of Adaptation wizard.

I would create an SAP ticket and ask them about this.

entry.title: Re: Configuring the Internal Hiring/Crossboarding Process in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello Shruthi,

This blog is amazing with all required inputs that ease the config part.

Would like to ask whether EC Workflow will get triggered if Crossboarding is done from RCM.

  • Which means, after completing Crossboarding (initiated from RCM), if there is a workflow in EC (such as workflow for transfer related event reasons), will those get triggered in EC ?

Please clarify.

Thank you,

Rajesh

entry.title: SAP career

My name is Ruth, I have years of experience in administrative role and currently work as sales admin. Am looking forward advancing my career in SAP SD but I notice there are lots of modules which get me confused.

 

Can anyone help me by attaching links of module that might be beneficial and in line to my field.

 

 

Thanks @comminity

entry.content:

We have CPI not PI/ PO. Planning to use MDR proxy for the consumer proxies to avoid any manual creation of WSDLs. 

Any bottlenecks or recommendations or guidelines that would help is appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Make OAA run with Spartacus UI Framework in SAP Commerce Cloud versions lower than 2105 - A How-
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to set up OAA in my checkout process as well. I’m familiar with the customized approach but the post I found seems outdated.

Could you tell me how the OAA setup looks in 2024? Specifically, is there a new or simplified way to set up OAA using Commerce 2211.25 with Spartacus, without the need for custom code, by utilizing the out-of-the-box extensions?

entry.title: /SCWM/POHUITM table not updated after the quantity adjustment (O001 Process Code)
entry.content:
Hi,

I have an ODO with only 1 item inside.

I change the quantity from 10 to 12 using the Process Code O001. (With Quantity Adjustment)

Things work fine but the table /SCWM/POHUITM is not updated with the new quantity value.

Is there any PPF action missing?

Any kind of idea is appreciated.

Best Regards

entry.title: Recommended implementation of a dependent branch abroad S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello experts,

My company is planning to set up a dependent branch abroad. Unfortunately, we are not sure how to implement this correctly in SAP.
Can all these points be mapped in a German company code (possibly via a ledger solution?) or does it make more sense to have different company codes?

Initial situation:
- S4 Public Cloud
- Company based in Germany
- Dependent branch office in France
- No own plant/warehouse in France
- Country-specific chart of accounts for France (mandatory)
- Own balance sheet preparation France (mandatory)
- Own tax code
- VAT in France
- Intrastat declaration
- ZM report
- Mapping of data in DE balance sheet by adding FR accounts
- Consolidation of intercompany transactions
- Drop shipment DE / (FR) / end customer

The tendency is to create a separate company code.

My buddies in my desk!

#devtoberfest #petoberfest

entry.title: Deliver Real World Results with SAP Business AI at SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
Are you ready to dive into the latest SAP Business AI innovations shaping the future of enterprise technology? SAP TechEd 2024 is right around the corner, bringing together developers, IT leaders, and the broader SAP community to share insights, learn, and build solutions that will drive tomorrow’s businesses. Whether you're interested in artificial intelligence, cloud solutions, or next-generation tools, SAP TechEd has something for everyone.

What to Expect

SAP TechEd offers a comprehensive selection of sessions tailored to meet the needs of developers, technology enthusiasts, and business leaders. These sessions provide valuable insights into SAP’s cutting-edge technologies and their impact on industries across the globe.

It’s a unique opportunity to engage with experts, developers, and IT leaders to learn new ways to advance your career and contribute to your company’s success.

Discover how innovations such as Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot, and large language models are transforming all business areas. Hear how SAP Business AI can enhance decision-making and agility while delivering tangible results.

Key SAP Business AI Sessions to Watch

KEY100CEST | SAP TechEd in 2024 Launch - Where ideas get real
 At SAP TechEd, experts, developers, IT leaders, and our SAP Community gather to learn, share, and build together. Hear firsthand from SAP Executives Muhammad Alam and Michael Ameling how innovations in SAP Build solutions, SAP Business AI, SAP BTP, and other solutions across our end-to-end portfolio are revolutionizing the tech world.

AI100 | Artificial intelligence that creates real-world results

Dive into the transformative potential of SAP Business AI and how you can use it in your daily work. Get an overview on the latest capabilities, and gain insights into more securely integrating AI into business applications, efficiently developing generative AI scenarios, and deploying the Joule copilot in your SAP software landscape. Explore essential capabilities, and elevate your AI strategy and business processes. Discover how SAP's advanced AI technologies can enhance your innovation.

PAR103 | Unlocking AI innovation with SAP and Microsoft solutions

Get ready to realize the full potential of AI innovation with SAP and Microsoft solutions. Explore the synergy between SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and Microsoft Azure AI, explore groundbreaking ways to integrate SAP S/4HANA with Microsoft Copilot, and discover how you can harness the power of Azure AI with SAP solutions. Elevate your technical expertise, and learn how to drive actionable insights and business innovation with game-changing AI technologies.

AI103 | Unlock generative AI more securely across your business with leading LLMs

Learn how to more securely and smoothly leverage market-leading large language models (LLMs) to enhance productivity, creativity, and efficiency throughout your organization. Discover how to integrate and orchestrate these models programmatically using the generative AI hub and other advanced capabilities in the SAP AI Core infrastructure and SAP AI Launchpad on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).

AD180 | Build extensions with ABAP Cloud and Joule's ABAP developer capabilities

Learn how to develop transactional SAP Fiori apps in ABAP Cloud using ABAP RESTful application programming model supported by generative AI. Explore features such as business events for loosely coupled integration scenarios and read-only "treeviews" for displaying hierarchical data. ABAP Cloud is our comprehensive development model for building clean-core-compliant apps, services, and extensions on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP S/4HANA, both in the cloud and on premise.

AI201 | Development with our generative AI hub, SDKs, and orchestration features

Learn to develop dynamic generative AI applications using large language models (LLMs) with the generative AI hub in the SAP AI Core infrastructure. Understand how to take advantage of powerful SDKs and orchestration capabilities to accelerate your time to market.

AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Discover the powerful synergy between Joule technology and SAP Build solutions that facilitates a smooth extension-building process. Learn how to enhance AI-powered capabilities in Joule with custom-developed enterprise skills tailored to your company's specific needs. By integrating with both SAP and third-party applications, Joule can instantly apply its newfound knowledge within SAP systems. Make your company more adaptive, responsive, and successful in the digital age.

AD107 | Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule's ABAP developer capabilities

Discover Joule's ABAP developer capabilities. Hear about detailed use cases, the generative AI road map, and insights on how these advancements can boost your productivity as a developer in your daily tasks.

AI105 | Examining how to onboard and extend Joule

Drive efficiency and productivity across your business by onboarding and extending Joule, our generative AI copilot, across your cloud solutions from SAP. Learn how to integrate Joule into SAP S/4HANA and SAP SuccessFactors solutions. Gain insights into the Joule road map, architecture, and preconfigured capabilities. Additionally, explore how Joule uses document grounding for more precise business answers, and learn how to extend this feature with your own HR policy document.

DT109 | Explore AI-driven ERP transformation with SAP Signavio solutions

Discover the future of SAP S/4HANA software transformations with the combined power of business process management and generative AI, leveraging the vast process data unlocked by SAP Signavio solutions. Our advanced process models enable quicker, simpler modeling, mining, and transformation, paving the way for innovative business optimization. Join us in pioneering the next era of SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution transformations.

AI107 | From chaos to clarity: Boost your business data with SAP AI Services

Discover how to maximize the potential of structured business data with SAP AI Services, such as Data Attribute Recommendation and Personalized Recommendation. And see firsthand how the Document Information Extraction service can revolutionize the handling of unstructured data right from the core input channels. The services act as a universal gateway, efficiently selecting and initiating relevant processes and integrating extracted results with structured data.

AI106 | Harness retrieval-augmented generation in Joule and generative AI hub

Gain a comprehensive understanding of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), a cutting-edge approach to natural language processing that significantly enhances the accuracy of generated text. Explore our innovative RAG architecture within the Joule copilot and the generative AI hub capability, and learn how to implement RAG to provide relevant, context-rich answers from your large language models (LLMs), effectively minimizing AI hallucinations.

AI101 | Enable more secure and responsible generative AI

Gain an in-depth exploration of SAP's ethical and responsible AI practices. Drawing on an overview of our AI ethics guidelines and procedures, we explore how developers can integrate AI ethics into their use cases. Through practical examples, find out how features such as content filtering and data masking within our generative AI hub can support the responsible use of AI, helping ensure adherence to ethical standards and practices.

AI180 | Build your own generative AI scenario for your use case

Get hands-on with the generative AI hub in the SAP AI Core infrastructure, and leverage market-leading large language models (LLMs) to build your own use case. You can take advantage of templating, content filtering, and powerful SDKs to accelerate your project. Walk through the technology components and methodologies, with our support each step of the way. Learn how to efficiently create robust generative AI scenarios.

AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators?

Discover how SAP Business AI empowers partners and customers to innovate and create value. We'll showcase the latest advancements in the generative AI hub and Joule capabilities and feature Agilita's experience using generative AI hub to develop high-impact solutions.

AI102 | Enhance business predictions with SAP Foundation Model and knowledge graphs

Discover how SAP Foundation Model, a table-native AI model, can accelerate time to value for predictive tasks on tabular data, addressing the limitations of large language models in business forecasting. Explore the integration of SAP Foundation Model and knowledge graphs, and see how explicit business semantics enhance business predictions.

Register Now! 

Don’t miss the chance to attend these and many more sessions live.

Register for SAP TechEd 2024 today and be part of the conversation that shapes the future of Business AI.

Click here to register.

Watch On-Demand

Missed a session? Don’t worry—following the live event, all sessions will be available on-demand so you can catch up on all the action at your convenience. Keep an eye out for the on-demand links post-event.

entry.title: Absence Quota Generation after 7 hours of overtime on time evaluation
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We have a requirement for automatic generation of Absence Quota Type 02 when the employee has worked overtime for 7 hours. I have checked the IMG for Generation Rules for Quota Type Selection and have some question about this configuration

1. How do i set the minimum hours worked before the quota is generated? i tried to look around but didn't find any field that indicate the minimum time balance needed.

2. How do i set the validity from of the quota as the generation date and the validity end as +3 after the generation date? i'm guessing the option would be transfer time but i want to make sure what's the differences between accrual period and transfer time

The use-case would be like this :

- On 24.09.2024 if the employee have a minimum of 7 hours overtime, the time evaluation will generate 1 Day of absence quota with validity date 24.09.2024-24.12.2024

Thanks and regard, Kelvin

entry.title: Re: An ABAP question about memory leak and object instances
entry.content: You should first make sure that there is a problem in garbage collection. If in doubt, run the garbage collector from time to time, it will free automatically the memory. If the reference of NEW is not stored in a variable, it means that it can be freed by the garbage collector.

entry.title: Re: Error while executing Bapi_Alm_Order_Maintain...

Sorry, new to the forum. I have found the solution. Have few other queries will ask a new question.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How can an independent consultant purchase a new 'red...

You should first navigate to sap.com to register. Please see further images below.

Once you have the account created and move to purchase Learning Hub, DCP will ask you to select if they are purchasing as a company or as individual.

Sheena

entry.title: How to check which Resource is assigned to a Work Package using CDS Views?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support,

I'm unable to get the data of Resources staffed to Projects.

We use the below mentioned steps:

  1. Create a Project;
  2. Request a Resource;
  3. Resource Assigned in C4P;
  4. Staffed Resource appears at Work Package level.

We need to capture the assigned resources against the Work Package.

Our question is: how to check which Resource is assigned to a Work Package using CDS Views?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check which Resource is assigned to a Work Pac...
entry.content:
Hello!

You can use the below CDS View to check the Resource (s) assigned to a Work Package in a Customer Project:

I_ResourceRequestPlanItem

Thanks,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Birthday and Anniversary alerts to the employees in SuccessFactors
entry.content: This was a fantastic guide!  It worked perfectly.  I do have a question, though, can this be expanded to tiles on the user/manager's homepage.  I know there is an offering of rule based tiles, so I am curious if there is any way to have this rule also put a tile on a homepgae.  Thanks again!

entry.title: Re: Create Extension Wizard to Create SAP UI5 Extension
entry.content:
You should not have to use Web IDE. Even if the app doesn't qualify for Adaptation project, the wizard in BAS still shows the app and creates an extension project for you, with a .extconfig.json.

Then you right-click on that and WebIde menus show up to let you extend the app. See this blog.

SAP's answer to your ticket is not an acceptable answer. See if you can get passed the first-level support and get a developer engaged.

entry.title: Document Change Rules, Line Item - Profit Center and Business Area
entry.content: I have mark field can be change for profit center and business area under "Document Change Rules, Line Item" but it is not allowed me to change the same. Please advise.

entry.title: Re: Asset transfer (value adjustment)
entry.content:
Well, if we have depreciation posting on the sender asset and we do a transfer, the deprecation information will come as adjustment value on the new asset. In your case the NAV at the time of transfer will remain though the asset life if you choose the depreciation key as 0000 in the new land asset.

If you don't want the adjustment value at all in the new land asset: You can retire the sender asset and create the new asset with the NAV (you will not have the linking  though).  

Second option is change the depr key to 0000 in the original asset and rerun the depreciation. This renders the asset to its original APC value. Next, you can do a partial transfer to the extent of the NAV you want on the new asset. Old asset can be retired after the transfer.

entry.title: Redis on SAP BTP - Instance sharing | 'reference' service plan
entry.content:
Redis instances can be shared between different environments and different accounts to enable reuse of instances. This feature provides the functionality of sharing a service instance between different sub-accounts.

Please consider:

  • You can only share service instances of the service plan ’standard’ or ’premium’. The sharing functionality only works between sub-accounts inside the same BTP region, the sharing works also between different global accounts, but the sub-accounts need to be in same region (e.g. EU10).
  • A reference service instance can be used like any "standard", "premium” instance, to be able to create it you need to use ‘reference’ service plan as entitlements.
  • You can bind or unbind it, create service keys etc., however, to modify database settings or configurations, you still need to use the source instance.

In order to create a Redis instance using a BTP Cockpit or CF CLI commands access the SAP Help Portal service documentation.

Update an active instance as ‘shareable’ using BTP Cockpit

A Redis instance can be shared between different accounts on the same landscape. As a  first step to use shared instances you need to setup the source instance with the list of sub-accounts that you allow to share the instances. Thus, you need to have at least one target sub-account available to add to the instance shares list.

Configure your instance as ‘shareable’

Pre-requisites:

  • To be able to share an instance you need to add the list of sub-accounts who want to consume it.
  • An instance can only be shared via an update process and not during the creation.

Go to the source sub-account where you have the instance and update the parameter "instance_shares" with the list of sub-accounts where you want to create the new instances using reference service plan.

 

{
    "instance_shares":{
    "op": "add",
    "values": [<”suba-account1”>]
    }
    }

 

The list of subaccounts is additive, so you do not need to always provide the complete list of subaccounts here. You can use the operations “op”:

  • “add” – add sub-account
  • “delete” – delete sub-account

In the update command parameters, you need to provide the list of subs-accounts, see below our JSON example:

 

{
    "instance_shares":{
    "op": "add",
    "values": [”1b73a8a4-357b-xxxx-8a37-1c245b83c443"]
    }
    }

 

In the source sub-account, go to your instance and select "update" option:

Add the JSON to parameters sections and select "update instance". Note: the update operation can only include this parameter.

Create a new ‘virtual’ instance referencing a source instance using BTP Cockpit.

To be able to create an instance using a shared reference instance, you need to have the proper entitlements in your target sub-account. 

1 - Confirm your entitlements (‘reference’ service plan) > When creating the new instance, you need to select the “reference” service plan thus ensure you have added this as an entitlement. 

2 - Create a new instance using ‘reference’ service plan > After selecting the “reference” service plan, you need to fill the rest of the inputs as you would with a normal instance creation.

3 - Add your source instance in the parameters > Select “next” as you will need to add the source instance id.

4 - Check the source instance id that you want to use as reference in the source sub-account.

5 - Add the instance id to the parameter “source_instance_id”

6 - After adding the "source_instance_id", you can select "create". Once created, you can check the new instance details and you will be able to see that was created using the "reference" service plan.

Optionally you may use the "Labels" feature to add some information for your future reference.

The 'source_instance_id’ parameter is visible in the instance parameters via BTP Cockpit or you can retrieve it via be below Cloud Foundry command line (CLI):

 

cf service <instance name> --params

 

Use Cloud Foundry CLI commands to create ‘virtual’ instances using a“shared” instance as a reference.

1 - Share a service instance

To share a service instance to a subaccount you can share the source instance using the below CF CLI command:

 

cf update-service ${source-instance-name} -c '{"instance_shares":{"op": "add", "values": ["<subaccount1>"]}}'

 

If the reference service instance has been deleted, you can also unshare the previously shared instance from the subaccount:

 

cf update-service ${source-instance-name} -c '{"instance_shares":{"op": "delete", "values": ["<subaccount2>"]}}'

 


2 - Create a new ‘virtual’ service instance

To create a reference instance for the Redis instance with the instance ID <source-instance-id>, please leverage the following CF CLI command:

 

cf create-service redis-cache reference reference-example -c '{"source_instance_id": "<source-instance-id>"}'

 

'reference’ is the service plan | ‘reference-example’ is the name of the new virtual instance to be created.

For more information on the Redis service please access the official Redis Help Portal service documentation

Thank you for reading!

SAP BTP Backing Services – Product Management

entry.content:
I'm looking forward to see everyone there.

For fun - here's a picture of @craigcmehil 's dinner last night.

From a local place called Thunderbirds

entry.title: Query to get date goods receive date vs inspection start date and inspection end date
entry.content:
Hi everyone

Ive recently started diving into SAP queries. I currently have access to my company's data lake for SAP data (meaning I don't have access to meaningful column headers). I am using xlean website to figure out tables and column names.

I have been asked to compare goods received vs inspection start and end dates. I am not sure the best approach but from searching around this forum i've come up with the following, but the PO_Posting_date seems to update whenever a new part gets received on that PO. Do you have any suggestions on how to look this information up?

 

Thanks in advance!

 

select ekbe.BELNR as material_doc_number, ekbe.BLDAT as doc_date, ekbe.BUDAT as PO_Posting_Date, ekbe.MENGE as quantity, ekbe.ebeln as purchase_order, ekbe.matnr as material_number, ekbe.werks as plant, qals.prueflos as inspection_lot_number, qals.art as inspection_Type, qals.stat35 as usage_decision_made, qals.ENSTEHDAT as inspection_lot_created_Date, qals.ENTSTEZEIT as inspection_lot_created_time, qals.PASTRTERM as inspection_lot_inspection_start_date, qals.PASTRZEIT as inspection_lot_inspection_start_time, qals.PAENDTERM as inspection_lot_inspection_end_date, qals.PAENDZEIT as inspection_lot_inspection_end_time, if(qals.pastrterm != qals.paendterm, 'not match', 'match') as compare_dates
from prod_l1.mbp.ekbe 
left join prod_l1.mbp.qals on qals.ebeln = ekbe.ebeln and qals.ebelp = ekbe.ebelp
--join prod_l1.mbp.QAMB on QAMB.mblnr = prod_l1.mbp.ekbe.belnr and QAMB.mjahr = prod_l1.mbp.ekbe.gjahr and QAMB.zeile = ekbe.buzei    
 where ekbe.werks = 'US9L' and ekbe.bewtp = 'E' 
 order by ekbe.src_commit_time desc

entry.title: Re: What are the restrictions in sap screen personas ?
entry.content: In the Public Cloud version of Personas, it is not possible to consume CDS views.

entry.title: Add custom fields to SAP Cloud Bill Of Lading "TM_US_BILL_LADING" form
entry.content:
We have a requirement to add fiew fields to the bill of lading form under SAP Cloud environment.

To be able to add those fields we probably have to create custom fields and associating it to the right business object.

But we have not find any where to associate the new custom fields to be able to see it in the FDP_TM_CMR_SRV service.

 

What are the step to be able to add custom fields to the Bill Of lading form?

Regards

Daniel

entry.content: That's correct.

entry.title: IW73 Plan Total Cost Glitch
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

In IW73 I have jobs that show a Plan Total Cost that goes up exponentially every time I refresh or log back in and access the order. I have jobs up to 58 million that started out at only 15,000. Is this a known bug or could it be from an external app? We use Salesforce too.

entry.title: Inappropriate Content in Candidate Application
entry.content:
We had a situation occur where one of our recruiters discovered an extremely inappropriate image uploaded by a candidate for hire in SuccessFactors Recruiting.  Apparently the candidate uploaded the wrong attachment by accident, as it was an extremely graphic. We are looking for options to prevent recurrence and protect our SFSF users. The recruiter had no prior warning or indication that the attachment was inappropriate.

Just to further clarify:

  1. This was a photograph in SFSF Recruiting, through the Resume Viewer. Viewing the image required downloading it, since it was not viewable in the Resume Viewer.
  2. The attachment didn’t contain viruses or malware, so typical anti-virus logic wouldn’t apply.

Is anyone aware of any existing functionality that might proactively catch something like this? Such as image-based AI recognition of a file that can’t be read through OCR, or perhaps recognize human skin in the image itself?

We discussed this situation with SuccessFactors support already. SFSF does not have this functionality currently or have any plans to do so. The closest thing I could find was Google's Cloud Vision API does have the SafeSearch functionality that could possibly catch this outside of SFSF.

Thanks in advance.

Jay

entry.title: Re: 999 – The Number of the BSEG
entry.content:
Thank you for your quick response 😀

We currently have document split enabled and that is why we have the problem. The customer invoice has more than 999 items and when doing the intercompany it generates us more than one document with the same reference number. When we send those invoices to the Spanish tax office, only one is accepted and the rest are rejected because the invoice is duplicated (same reference number that must match the number of the sales invoice issued). This is the reason why the split document is not useful for us and we need to summarize the accounting document items.

Regards

Juan Pedro

entry.content:

We have blog for CID add on. I think it will help you  https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/ariba-cig-add-on-series-cig-overview/ba-p/13358724

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: SAP career
entry.content: SD is an on premise product; SAP's future is in the cloud.  Have you considered taking SAP Sales/Service cloud free training here: https://learning.sap.com/products/customer-experience/sales-cloud

entry.title: CDN Integration with Accelerator based storefront
entry.content:
Wherever there is any redirection triggered from the the Commerce pods, we observed that HttpServletRequestWrapper.getServerName() does not return the expected Akamai domain but instead returns the internal server name that corresponds to the storefront domain url defined in the cloud portal. 

Can you please provide some guidance on how we can set up HttpServletRequestWrapper.getServerName() to return the CDN domain?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CDN Integration with Accelerator based storefront
entry.content:
HttpServletRequestWrapper.getServerName() does not return the CDN domain by default. The method getServerName() in the HttpServletRequest (or HttpServletRequestWrapper which extends it) returns the server name from the HTTP request, which typically corresponds to the host value from the Host header in the request.

Here's how it works:

  • Direct Request: If the client sends a direct request to your server, getServerName() will return the name of your server (e.g., www.yourdomain.com).
  • Request through CDN: If the request is made via a CDN (like Cloudflare, Akamai or AWS CloudFront), the CDN will forward the request to your server, and the value of getServerName() will be whatever was in the Host header sent by the client. This is usually the original domain name requested by the client, not the CDN's domain.

However, if your CDN is rewriting headers (for example, changing the Host header), or if you have specific rules set in the CDN configuration to change the Host header, the behaviour could vary. But under normal circumstances, the method returns the host that the client originally requested, which is typically your own domain.

If you need the actual CDN domain or IP, you might need to check other headers like X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Host, or CDN-specific headers.

entry.title: Re: Inverse/InverseIF formula in sap analytics cloud
entry.content: Depending on the business model, prices may fluctuate over time. How would you handle the correct weighted average aggregation over Date and still allow meaningful planning? (e.g. changes in units should not change the unit cost but rather the revenues).

entry.title: Re: Failed to get OData service metadata document. Please...
entry.content: Worst case you can manually update the metadata in your MDK project by downloading the $metadata and replacing the .xml for the service in the Services folder.

entry.title: Re: How to use an XSUAA instance together with a .NET application

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports License
entry.content:
For development only 1 copy of Crystal Reports is required per Developer.

For distribution for external users see this page:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2015/07/368edae4-597c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

Basically each site requires a copy of Crystal Reports.

And keep your application runtime up to date, you can download it from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

Here's a blog I wrote for upgrading:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

Which has good info for each update, distribution etc....

entry.title: Re: payment term field displayed; business user want edit...
entry.content:
Hi 

To edit display and optional entry in purchase order, you need to go to spro- material management- purchasing- purchase order 

check which screen layout has been defined at document level and then go to screen layout at document level option make it edit 

It will work for you 

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: AIF monitoring in SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are currently using SAP PO/PI 7.5 as Integration platform with in our landscape..

To do AIF(application interface framework) monitoring, to see the end to end message flow from/to S4/ECC regarding outbound/inbound interfaces we have customized Fiori page...

we are using the Fiori page for the following cases..

(i) AIF Messages (ii) AIF Monitoring (iii) Interface monitoring...

In that Fiori page, it shows status of the messages on SAP backend & SAP PO/PI.. also if someone clicks on SAP PO status "hyperlink" of that specific message then it re-directs to the SAP PO monitoring page of that specific message... it is possible to link SAP backend and SAP PO messsage on Fiori page because message ID for outbound messages is same on SAP backend & SAP PO/PI.. it is easier to fetch SAP PO status of the specific message...

Fiori page with SAP PO Message status Hyperlink (here "Success" is hyperlink)..

SAP PO monitoring page of that specific message

We are planning to migrate all the interfaces from SAP PO/PI to SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration)..

Now, what are different alternate options available within SAP BTP to cater this requirement..

1. BTP Based Fiori app 

2. SAP Cloud ALM for end to end monitoring..

AFAIK, the option 1) above is expensive since Fiori developers has to put of lot of efforts to build such app for AIF monitoring.. Could option 2) completely replace the above solution implemented in SAP PO/PI?? Could you please let me know which is better option in terms various factors like Cost, efforts, time to develope/implement, SAP recommendation, standard solution etc.., Thank you!

//Ramu

entry.title: Re: Unidade Basica de Medida - Mestre de Material SAP 4/HANA
entry.content: Boa Tarde

entry.title: Re: Query to get date goods receive date vs inspection start date and inspection end date
entry.content:
Try joins such as

QAMB
|__ QALS
|__ MSEG
      |__ MKPF
      |__ EKBE
             |__ EKPO

entry.title: Re: Runtime for crystal reports 14.2.x on the server
entry.content:
Get the latest CR for VS from here, and please keep it up to date, CR for VS releases about 2 times a year:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

See my blog ion upgrading your project.

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

You must update your Reports to CR 2016 or CR 2020, CR 8.5 used legacy database drivers and they will not work with the latest drivers. You will need to update each report manually using CR Designer by selecting Set Database Location. The RPT files keeps internally the client dll used and only way to update them is to use the Designer.

The TLC error is due to mixed runtime files, CR 8.5 cannot be installed on the same PC as current versions.

entry.title: Re: CIM- Unable to submit invoices SCIM, system is showing error message a technical error occurred.
entry.content: Were you able to resolve this issue?

entry.title: Re: how to transform xml from one format to other
entry.content:
You can use below XSLT code to covert in required format

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
<xsl:output method="xml" indent="yes"/>

<xsl:template match="/Values">
<Values>
<xsl:for-each select="Value">
<xsl:element name="{@AttributeID}">
<Changed><xsl:value-of select="@Changed"/></Changed>
<ID><xsl:value-of select="@ID"/></ID>
<text><xsl:value-of select="."/></text>
</xsl:element>
</xsl:for-each>
</Values>
</xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

entry.title: Re: Disaggregation Expression when Aggr level is NULL
entry.content:
Hello Yogananda,

unfortunately this solution does not work. This expression does not decide on aggregate level whether KF1 or KF2 should be used as disaggregation factor in general. It decides for each object on the base planning level. This mixes the disaggregation factor. I have the same problem as the questioner. Apparently there is currently no solution to this problem.

Kind regards

entry.title: controller and firefighter cannot be the same person
entry.content:
getting error "controller and firefighter cannot be the same person" when FF user trying to return the request to FF Controller by providing additional information as requested by controller

The error is invalid. FF user is not tagged as controller for any id

Both controller and FF user type is maintained as dialog only

Given all authorization as per previous error screen shots and even tried with SAP_ALL but, same issue.

entry.title: Issue with uploading multiple line items data of Purhase Orders.
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are facing the problem to get all the items data of purchase order into process intelligence. we are uploading the flat csv files, and our input file has all the items with respective to their purchase order. but, after uploading when we are trying the fetch the data into widget of diff po's and their items. it is fetching only latest line item and skipping all the previous one.

ex - For PO - 4500433959 I have 2-line item - 10 and 20. after upload in PI model it is showing only 20 and same happing with other PO's

Kindly let us know if anyone has encountered such a problem and how you did fix it. Your response are much appreciated.

entry.title: Re: VA05 - limit of records (250 000 records)

entry.title: Real Talk with Shilpa & Ananth | Integration Track Leads
entry.content:
We are Shilpa Vij and Ananth Natrajan, Product Managers for SAP BTP Integration Suite. We look forward to the upcoming SAP TechEd Virtual, especially the SAP BTP Integration track. 

Shilpa Vij: I am a passionate dancer and home decor enthusiast, and by profession, I am a Product Manager. I have co-authored two books. Currently, my interest lies in exploring, learning, and showcasing "product management" as an art.  

Ananth Natarajan: I’m a great enthusiast of nature and love every creature of nature. Apart from my Product Manager role in the SAP Integration Suite team, I prefer to travel and explore different places within my reach. 

The Integration track provides an in-depth overview of SAP's powerful integration tools and solutions, focusing on seamlessly connecting applications, data, business partners, and processes across diverse environments. This track covers topics such as event-driven architecture, API management, hybrid integration, and migration strategies, enabling businesses to drive agility, efficiency, and innovation. Through expert-led sessions, attendees will explore real-world use cases and best practices, gaining valuable insights into leveraging SAP Integration Suite to support digital transformation and optimize business outcomes. 

What inspired you to specialize in this track? 

Explore a diverse array of topics, including AI in Enterprise Integration, API Management, Event-driven integration, Integration Advisor, Trading Partner Management, Migration Accelerators to move to the Cloud, Edge Integration Cell, Enterprise Automation with SAP, and beyond. We’re thrilled to work with such exceptional speakers and product managers. Stay tuned for exciting updates and feature releases that we'll be sharing at TechEd! 

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

Through jump-start sessions, real-world case studies, and interactive discussions, participants learn how to bring their integration ideas to life using the SAP Integration Suite. The track focuses on practical, scalable approaches that empower businesses to solve complex integration challenges, streamline operations, and enable seamless connectivity across cloud and on-premise environments—turning visionary ideas into impactful realities. 

Stay tuned for jumpstart sessions 

IN180 Future-proof your SAP PI/PO investments with SAP Integration Suite 

IN280 Use SAP Integration Suite with SAP IBP and third-party systems  

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar):  

  • IN200 | Unleash the power of AI in enterprise integrations 
  •  IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite 
  • IN101 | Leverage real-time business insights with an event-driven architecture 
  • IN102 | Take control of your APIs with the API Management and Graph capabilities 
  • IN105 | Unleash the power of AI-driven enterprise automation with SAP solutions 

To gain an overview of the SAP Integration Suite, including the latest innovations and roadmap, we encourage you to attend the session 

IN100 | Innovate now with a modern, AI-assisted integration suite 

 What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024? 

  • A coffee cup for early morning keynotes! 
  • The device of choice: Whether it’s your laptop, tablet, or phone, you can tune into TechEd from wherever you are this year! 
  • Bring a spirit of exploration and eagerness to learn: TechEd is your chance to dive into cutting-edge technologies. Be ready to connect with SAP experts, ask questions, and soak up all the knowledge you can!  

Join us in the Integration track and be part of a vibrant community eager to explore the future of seamless connectivity. Dive into sessions that will equip you with the tools and knowledge to master the SAP Integration Suite and discover how it can elevate your business processes. Don't miss this opportunity to engage with experts, exchange ideas, and learn from hands-on demos. Register now and let's unlock new possibilities together! Explore TechEd 2024 session catalog!

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: How to find the Manage My Substitutes feature in My Inbox app?
entry.content:
Dear SAP team,

 

I want to set a substitute with the app My Inbox (F0862).

How can I navigate to "Manage My Substitutes" screen?

 

I don´t get this button displayed in my inbox.

 

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to find the Manage My Substitutes feature in My I...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

To open "Manage My Substitutes" you need to click on your Profile Icon and then, in the dropdown, choose "Manage My Substitutes". 

 

Kind regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Intrastat 201 - Scenario-Specific Roles and Responsibilities
entry.content:
Trond, 

do you know?

if i have a plant in the US in one state, and a subcontractor is assigned to that same plant but has an address in another state, and the subcontractor sends the finished product to a depot in a third state, how would SAP handle the tax determination? Specifically, do i need to create the subcontractor in its own plant to make this work?

And for europe (intrastat), does this work?

Intrastat:

Same as above, but now involving EU countries instead of US states

entry.title: Re: Restricting Coupon Usage to 1 per B2B Unit in SAP Commerce
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions. Hopefully someone will fix it for you.
